# Financial Deep Research: Vistra Corp (VST)

Research conducted: 2026-05-21
Stage 1 baseline provided: No — §9 notes the absence.

## 1. Recent Financial Performance & Quarterly Trends

### Most Recent Report
Vistra reported first-quarter 2026 results on May 7, 2026. For the quarter ended March 31, 2026, the company recorded GAAP net income of $1,029 million on operating revenues of $5,640 million, compared with a net loss of $(268) million on revenues of $3,933 million in the prior-year period. Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA was $1,494 million versus $1,240 million in Q1 2025. Cash flow from operations increased to $1,200 million from $599 million in the year-ago quarter. Capital expenditures ranged from $576 million to $883 million in the quarter (per supplemental disclosure). [Source: Company Q1 earnings / Title: Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

**Metric substitution note:** For an integrated competitive power generation and retail electricity company, GAAP net income is heavily distorted by unrealized mark-to-market gains and losses on commodity derivatives used to hedge power and fuel exposures. In Q1 2026, for example, net income included a $723 million unrealized gain from hedges expected to settle in future years; in Q1 2025, the company recorded unrealized losses that contributed to the net loss. Accordingly, this analysis emphasizes **Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA** and **Adjusted Free Cash Flow before Growth (FCFbG)** as the most meaningful measures of operating performance, consistent with management’s guidance framework and standard practice among independent power producers.

### Most Recent Full Year Report
For full-year 2025, Vistra reported GAAP net income of $944 million, down from $2,812 million in FY2024. Operating revenues were $17,738 million compared with $17,224 million in the prior year. Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA rose to $5,912 million from $5,643 million, and Ongoing Operations Adjusted FCFbG increased to $3,592 million from $2,888 million. Cash flow from operations was $4,070 million in 2025 versus $4,563 million in 2024. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Title: Vistra Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-26] ***

The decline in reported net income from 2024 to 2025 was driven primarily by an $808 million unrealized loss from hedges expected to settle in future years, versus more favorable mark-to-market impacts in 2024. On an operating basis, EBITDA and cash flow improved year over year.

### Key Business Drivers in Recent Quarters
First-quarter 2026 results were driven by higher realized energy and capacity prices, particularly in the East segment, and the first full-quarter contribution from the 2,600 MW Lotus natural gas acquisition closed in November 2025. These positives were partly offset by unfavorable retail segment results attributed to one of the mildest first quarters in Texas history, which reduced heating demand. Unrealized derivative marks swung significantly positive compared to the prior year, explaining the GAAP net income variance. [Source: Company Q1 earnings / Title: Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

### Segment Performance
Adjusted EBITDA by segment for the most recent periods:

**Q1 2026 vs Q1 2025:**
- Retail: $68 million (down from $184 million)
- Texas: $586 million (up from $490 million)
- East: $801 million (up from $514 million)
- West: $56 million (down from $62 million)
- Corporate and Other: $(17) million
- Asset Closure: $(19) million
- **Ongoing Operations Consolidated: $1,494 million** (up from $1,240 million)

**FY2025 vs FY2024:**
- Retail: $1,622 million (up from $1,463 million)
- Texas: $1,834 million (down from $2,032 million)
- East: $2,282 million (up from $2,017 million)
- West: $244 million (up from $225 million)
- Corporate and Other: $(70) million
- Asset Closure: $(74) million
- **Ongoing Operations Consolidated: $5,912 million** (up from $5,643 million)

[Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***; [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***

The East segment has become the largest contributor, reflecting the March 2024 Energy Harbor nuclear addition, the Lotus acquisition, and strong capacity price realizations. Texas remains a major profit center but declined in FY2025 due to market price normalization after strong prior-year scarcity pricing.

### Geographic & FX Exposure
Vistra operates entirely within U.S. competitive power markets (ERCOT, PJM, ISO-NE, NYISO, CAISO, MISO). It has no material foreign currency exposure. The retail business is concentrated in Texas and the PJM region. Load growth and power demand from data center expansion are currently most discussed in Texas and PJM/Mid-Atlantic markets. [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***

## 2. Growth Trajectory

### Trajectory Assessment
Vistra’s growth trajectory over the last two years has been dominated by M&A and contracted capacity additions rather than organic same-store volume growth:

- **FY2024:** Closed the $6.8 billion Energy Harbor transaction (4,048 MW nuclear, retail customers) on March 1, 2024. Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA of $5,656 million. [Source: Company FY2024 earnings / Date: 2025-02-27] ***; [Source: RBC Capital Markets / Title: $6.8bn sale of Energy Harbor to Vistra / Date: 2024-03-01] *
- **FY2025:** Closed Lotus acquisition (2,600 MW natural gas) in November 2025 for approximately $1.9 billion. Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA rose to $5,912 million (+4.7% year over year). [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***
- **FY2026 (guided):** Management guided Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA to $6.8 billion–$7.6 billion, representing 15%–29% growth over FY2025 at the respective midpoints. The guidance excludes any contribution from the pending Cogentrix acquisition. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***
- **FY2027:** Management has communicated an Adjusted EBITDA “midpoint opportunity” range of $7.4 billion–$7.8 billion based on October 2025 market curves, excluding Cogentrix and the recently signed Meta/AWS nuclear PPAs. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

The trajectory is sharply upward, but the two-year look-back window is insufficient to assess organic trend because Energy Harbor fundamentally re-based the earnings profile in 2024. On a same-fleet basis, growth is driven by higher realized capacity prices, hedge roll-off into higher forward curves, nuclear production tax credits (PTC), and data-center-linked PPA executions.

### Peer-Relative Growth Context
NRG Energy, the closest integrated competitor, reported FY2025 Adjusted EBITDA of $4,087 million and has also been growing via acquisition (13 GW from LS Power). Vistra’s FY2025 EBITDA of $5,912 million is roughly 45% larger, and its 2026 guided midpoint ($7.2 billion) implies a steeper forward growth trajectory, partly due to a heavier nuclear baseload position benefiting from IRA tax credits and scarcity pricing. [Source: NRG press release / Title: NRG Energy Reports Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-24] **

## 3. Margin & Profitability Profile

For Vistra, **gross margin and operating margin** are less informative than **Adjusted EBITDA margin and unit economics** ($/kW, $/MWh) because fuel and purchased power costs are pass-through or hedged items that create revenue volatility without proportional margin impact. The relevant profitability lens is the conversion of generation and retail gross margin to EBITDA after fixed costs.

- FY2025 operating income was $1,906 million on $17,738 million of revenue (10.7% operating margin), down from $4,081 million in FY2024, largely due to a $228 million long-lived asset impairment and higher operating/depreciation costs from the expanded fleet. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***
- FY2025 Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA of $5,912 million implies an adjusted margin of roughly 33% against operating revenues. This is broadly in line with the low-to-mid-30s gross margin levels reported in financial data terminals for the preceding year. [Source: WSJ / Title: Vistra Financials / Date: 2025-03-04] *

**Peer-Relative Profitability Context**
Vistra’s integrated model (wholesale generation + retail load) generally produces more stable EBITDA margins than pure-play independent power producers because the retail book provides a natural demand hedge. Compared with regulated utilities trading at 20%+ regulated ROEs, Vistra’s returns are more volatile but its recent Adjusted ROIC (8.6% per data-provider estimates) suggests it is earning near or above its cost of capital on invested capital, though this metric is noisy due to purchase accounting and derivative impacts. [Source: WSJ / Title: Vistra Financials / Date: 2025-03-04] *

## 4. Cash Generation & Quality

### Cash Generation Quality Assessment
Vistra’s preferred cash metric is **Ongoing Operations Adjusted FCFbG**, which management defines as cash from operations less maintenance capex, environmental credits, and working capital adjustments, before growth investments. This metric filters out lumpy growth capex (solar, battery, gas development) and working capital swings tied to margin deposits.

- FY2024: $2,888 million
- FY2025: $3,592 million (+24% year over year)
- FY2026 guidance: $3.925 billion–$4.725 billion

[Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***; [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

Cash flow quality appears **moderate to high** for the sector. The generation fleet produces tangible cash backed by physical capacity; however, a material portion of EBITDA is supported by the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) nuclear Production Tax Credit (PTC). In FY2024, Vistra recognized $545 million of transferable nuclear PTC revenues and sold $200 million of transferable PTCs in January 2025. The PTC provides a revenue floor (up to $15/MWh, inflation-adjusted, phasing out as power prices rise above $25/MWh) through 2032. This is durable cash, but it carries regulatory/policy risk. [Source: Company 10-Q / Title: Q1 2025 Quarterly Report / Date: 2025-05-02] ***

### Working Capital & Cash Conversion
Working capital is **material** to Vistra’s business model but managed primarily through collateral and margin mechanisms. The company holds customer deposits and posts/receives margin against derivative positions. Unbilled retail revenues were $737 million as of March 31, 2025. Bad debt expense is modest ($44 million in Q1 2025) relative to retail scale. [Source: Company 10-Q / Title: Q1 2025 Quarterly Report / Date: 2025-05-02] ***

The cash conversion cycle is not meaningfully comparable to industrial or consumer businesses. Instead, liquidity management focuses on available credit facility capacity and collateral requirements. As of March 31, 2026, total available liquidity was approximately $4,173 million, including $634 million cash, $2,126 million corporate revolver availability, and $1,413 million commodity-linked revolver availability. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

### Earnings Quality
Reported GAAP earnings are **low quality** due to large unrealized mark-to-market swings. In FY2025, an $808 million unrealized loss from hedges reduced reported net income; in Q1 2026, a $723 million unrealized gain increased it. These are non-cash items that reverse as hedges settle. Adjusted EBITDA and FCFbG are better proxies for economic performance. Depreciation and amortization is elevated ($1,986 million in FY2025) due to purchase accounting from Energy Harbor and other acquisitions, which suppresses GAAP net income but does not affect cash generation. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***

## 5. Balance Sheet & Liquidity

As of March 31, 2026, long-term debt including current maturities was approximately $19,163 million, up from $17,043 million at year-end 2025, reflecting the Lotus acquisition financing and other refinancing activity. Cash and equivalents were $634 million. Enterprise value was approximately $69.48 billion against a market capitalization of $50.11 billion, implying net debt of roughly $19.4 billion. [Source: Stock Analysis / Title: VST Statistics & Valuation / Date: 2026-05-21] *; [Source: Stock Titan / Title: VST 10-Q filing summary / Date: 2026-05-07] *

### Debt Maturity Profile & Refinancing Risk
There is a **material discrepancy in reported near-term maturities** that warrants attention:

- The FY2025 10-K stated long-term debt maturities in 2026 of $1,201 million as of December 31, 2025. [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***
- However, Fitch Ratings reported in April 2026 that Vistra has debt maturities of $3.2 billion in 2026 (including an optional preferred stock redemption), $3.4 billion in 2027, and $786 million in 2028. [Source: Fitch Ratings / Title: Fitch Rates Vistra's Senior Unsecured Notes 'BBB-' / Date: 2026-04-08] **

The divergence likely reflects reclassifications, the timing of the Lotus acquisition close, or inclusion of preferred equity redemption obligations in the Fitch calculation that are classified differently under GAAP. Regardless, Vistra has prefunded a portion of 2026 obligations: in October 2025, it issued $750 million of 4.300% senior secured notes due 2028, $500 million of 4.600% due 2030, and $750 million of 5.250% due 2035, using proceeds to refinance senior unsecured debt maturing in September 2026 and to fund a portion of the Lotus acquisition. [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***

Credit ratings are split:
- **S&P:** BBB- (upgraded December 2025) [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***
- **Fitch:** BBB- (upgraded April 2026) [Source: Fitch Ratings / Title: Fitch Rates Vistra's Senior Unsecured Notes 'BBB-' / Date: 2026-04-08] **
- **Moody's:** B2 (downgraded May 2025, outlook stable) [Source: Investing.com / Title: Vistra Holdings ratings downgraded to B2 by Moody's / Date: 2025-05-20] *

Vistra is now investment grade at two of three major agencies. Fitch cited the company’s progress strengthening the balance sheet and providing visibility into longer-term earnings power as rationale for the upgrade. [Source: Fitch Ratings / Date: 2026-04-08] **

### Assessment
Leverage remains elevated for a non-utility power company. Debt/EBITDA was approximately 2.93x and interest coverage 3.72x per data-provider estimates as of May 2026. [Source: Stock Analysis / Title: VST Statistics & Valuation / Date: 2026-05-21] * The company has stated a long-term net leverage target of less than 3.0x. With FY2025 net leverage likely near or slightly above that threshold and $4+ billion of near-term maturities per Fitch, refinancing execution is a load-bearing assumption. The investment-grade upgrades should facilitate market access, but the Moody's split rating and the scale of pending growth capex (Cogentrix, nuclear uprates) keep balance-sheet risk on the radar.

## 6. Capital Deployment & Returns on Capital

### Share Repurchases
Vistra has executed an aggressive return-of-capital program. As of May 1, 2026, the company had repurchased approximately $6.3 billion of stock since November 2021, reducing shares outstanding by roughly 30% from the November 2021 base to approximately 337 million shares. In FY2025, Vistra repurchased 6.6 million shares for approximately $1.0 billion. The Board authorized an incremental $1.0 billion in October 2025, leaving roughly $1.5 billion available under the program as of early May 2026, which management expects to complete by year-end 2027. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***; [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***

### Dividends
The company pays a modest quarterly dividend of $0.23 per share ($0.91 annualized), yielding approximately 0.6% at recent prices. FY2025 common dividends totaled $306 million. Management has targeted $300 million in aggregate annual common dividends from 2024 through 2026. [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***; [Source: Stock Analysis / Title: VST Statistics & Valuation / Date: 2026-05-21] *

### M&A / Capex
Vistra’s capital deployment is heavily skewed toward **accretive generation acquisitions**:
- **Energy Harbor (closed March 2024):** Total acquisition date fair value of $5,407 million; $3,100 million cash consideration. Added 4,048 MW nuclear and ~1 million retail customers. [Source: SEC filing / Title: Business Combinations disclosure / Date: 2024-06-30] ***
- **Vistra Vision minority buyout (closed December 2024):** Undiscounted purchase price of $3,248 million to buy out Nuveen and Avenue Capital, making Vistra the sole owner of the nuclear, retail, and Vistra Zero assets. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Vistra to become sole owner of Vistra Vision / Date: 2024-09-19] *
- **Lotus (closed November 2025):** ~$1.9 billion for 2,600 MW of natural gas generation. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***
- **Cogentrix (pending, expected mid-to-late 2026):** ~$4.0 billion net purchase price ($2.3 billion cash, ~$0.9 billion stock at $185/share, assumption of ~$1.5 billion debt less ~$0.7 billion NPV of tax benefits) for 5,500 MW of modern natural gas capacity. Implies ~7.25x 2027 expected Adjusted EBITDA contribution. [Source: Company press release / Title: Vistra Adds to its Industry-Leading Generation Portfolio with Acquisition of Cogentrix / Date: 2026-01-05] ***; [Source: Morningstar / Title: Vistra: Cogentrix Acquisition Another Timely Deal / Date: 2026-01-06] **
- **Nuclear uprates/PPAs:** Signed long-term PPAs with Amazon Web Services (~3,800 MW at Comanche Peak) and Meta (PJM nuclear facilities). The Meta agreements support subsequent license renewal applications for four PJM nuclear units. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***

Management expects the Cogentrix deal to deliver mid-single-digit Ongoing Operations AFCFbG per share accretion in 2027 and high-single-digit average accretion over 2027–2029. [Source: Company press release / Date: 2026-01-05] ***

### Returns on Capital
Return metrics are distorted by acquisition accounting and derivative volatility. Data-provider estimates as of late 2024 showed Return on Equity of ~29% and Return on Invested Capital of ~8.6%. [Source: WSJ / Title: Vistra Financials / Date: 2025-03-04] * The ROE figure is inflated by a modest equity base ($5.6 billion book value at Dec 2024) relative to debt-heavy capitalization. The 8.6% ROIC estimate suggests Vistra is likely earning around or slightly above its weighted average cost of capital, though this is highly sensitive to commodity price assumptions and the carrying value of acquired assets. The 7.25x EBITDA multiple for Cogentrix implies disciplined capital allocation, but the aggregate pace of M&A ($10+ billion deployed since 2023) raises execution risk.

## 7. Management Guidance & Forward Outlook

### Most Recent Full Year Report Guidance
For 2026, management reaffirmed:
- **Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA:** $6.8 billion–$7.6 billion
- **Ongoing Operations Adjusted FCFbG:** $3.925 billion–$4.725 billion

These ranges exclude any contribution from the pending Cogentrix acquisition. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

### Most Recent Report Guidance
No change to 2026 guidance was made with the Q1 2026 earnings release. Management maintained the previously communicated 2027 Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA midpoint opportunity range of $7.4 billion–$7.8 billion, based on October 2025 market curves. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

### Management Commentary
CEO Jim Burke emphasized that load growth remains strong across primary markets and that a large, diversified, dispatchable generation fleet is essential to meeting demand and supporting reliability. He cited the integrated model and disciplined execution as positioning the company to deliver reliable power and long-term value. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

### Forward Claim Confidence Weighting
- **2026 Adjusted EBITDA and FCFbG guidance: HIGH confidence.** With approximately 98% of expected 2026 generation volumes hedged as of May 1, 2026, the guidance range is largely contracted. The primary residual uncertainty is weather-driven retail load and operational availability. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***
- **2027 Adjusted EBITDA midpoint opportunity ($7.4B–$7.8B): MEDIUM confidence.** This is described as an “opportunity” based on October 2025 forward curves, not formal guidance. It assumes favorable capacity price resets, continued retail performance, and no material market design changes. Hedge coverage of 89% for 2027 supports visibility but leaves more room for curve movement than 2026. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***
- **Cogentrix close and 2027 accretion: MEDIUM confidence.** The transaction is subject to FERC, HSR, and state regulatory approvals. While management has a strong track record integrating assets (Dynegy, Energy Harbor), the $4 billion scale and geographic dispersion across PJM, ISO-NE, and ERCOT create execution risk. [Source: Company press release / Date: 2026-01-05] ***
- **Long-term nuclear PTC economics and data-center load growth: MEDIUM-LOW confidence.** The nuclear PTC is statutorily available through 2032, but Treasury regulations and potential legislative changes create uncertainty. Data-center load growth is a visible tailwind but depends on macro AI capex cycles and transmission interconnection timelines that are outside Vistra’s control.

## 8. Material Financial Risks & One-Off Items

- **Commodity and hedge mark-to-market volatility:** GAAP earnings swing materially with forward power and gas prices. While cash economics are hedged, reported results can diverge significantly from operating cash flow. Q1 2026 net income included $723 million of unrealized hedge gains; FY2025 included $808 million of unrealized losses. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***; [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***
- **Regulatory and market design risk:** Vistra’s competitive generation model is exposed to changes in ISO/RTO market rules, capacity market reforms, and environmental regulations. The 10-K notes that pending or proposed laws under the Trump administration could materially affect the business, as could repeal of beneficial regulations. [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***
- **Winter Storm Uri litigation:** Following Winter Storm Uri (2021), numerous lawsuits were filed against generators. In December 2023, the First Court of Appeals granted Vistra’s mandamus petition and instructed the multi-district litigation court to grant generator defendants’ motions to dismiss. Plaintiffs have petitioned the Texas Supreme Court for review. A negative ruling could create material liability, though management believes defenses are strong. [Source: Company 10-Q / Title: Q1 2025 Quarterly Report / Date: 2025-05-02] ***
- **Nuclear operational and decommissioning risk:** The 6.4 GW nuclear fleet (Comanche Peak, Beaver Valley, Davis-Besse, Perry) carries operational, regulatory, and catastrophic-event risk. The Moss Landing battery storage incident (prior years) also highlighted storage asset risk. [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***
- **Interest rate and refinancing exposure:** With total debt near $19 billion, rising rates increase interest expense ($1,179 million in FY2025, up from $900 million in FY2024). Investment-grade upgrades should help, but the Moody's split rating and large 2026–2027 maturity walls require active management. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***; [Source: Fitch Ratings / Date: 2026-04-08] **
- **Retail weather and credit risk:** Mild weather in Q1 2026 reduced retail EBITDA by a material but unquantified amount relative to plan. The retail segment also faces competitive pressure and bad debt exposure ($75 million allowance as of March 2025). [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***; [Source: Company 10-Q / Title: Q1 2025 Quarterly Report / Date: 2025-05-02] ***

## 9. Discrepancies vs. Structured Data Baseline

### Research Data vs. Stage 1 Baseline
**Stage 1 baseline not provided at runtime — section deferred.** No structured data baseline was included in the research request. All findings above are derived from primary filings, earnings releases, and credible financial data providers as of the research date.

## 10. Key Financial Assumptions Embedded in Current Valuation

As of May 21, 2026, Vistra trades at approximately $148.60 per share, a $50.11 billion market capitalization, and an enterprise value of ~$69.5 billion. The valuation ratios include a forward P/E of ~16.1x, EV/EBITDA of ~10.2x on trailing metrics, and a PEG ratio of 0.34. [Source: Stock Analysis / Title: VST Statistics & Valuation / Date: 2026-05-21] *

For the current valuation to be justified, the following financial assumptions must largely hold:
1. **2026 guidance achievement:** The market is pricing in at least the midpoint of the $6.8B–$7.6B Adjusted EBITDA range and the $3.925B–$4.725B FCFbG range.
2. **Cogentrix closes and performs:** The ~$4 billion acquisition must close in mid-to-late 2026 and deliver the projected 7.25x EBITDA multiple and mid-single-digit 2027 FCF accretion.
3. **2027 EBITDA midpoint opportunity realization:** The stock appears to discount a path to the $7.4B–$7.8B “opportunity” range in 2027, implying sustained high power/capacity prices and successful execution of Meta/AWS nuclear PPAs.
4. **Continued aggressive buybacks:** The valuation likely assumes completion of the remaining $1.5 billion authorization by year-end 2027 and likely further extensions, given the 30% share-count reduction to date.
5. **Nuclear PTC durability:** Investors appear to assume the IRA nuclear PTC remains intact and monetizable through the decade, providing a material revenue floor.
6. **Leverage stabilization below 3.0x net debt/EBITDA:** The investment-grade trajectory assumes management hits its net leverage target while funding growth.
7. **Data-center/power demand supercycle:** The forward multiple embeds conviction that load growth from AI/data centers will tighten market conditions and support long-term capacity pricing in PJM, ERCOT, and ISO-NE.

If any of these assumptions fracture—particularly Cogentrix execution, 2027 market curve collapse, or repeal of nuclear PTCs—the bear case has clear quantitative targets to attack.

## 11. Bottom-Line Financial Assessment

### Business Model Characterization
Vistra is a **capital-intensive, commodity-exposed integrated power platform** with a unique hybrid profile: it combines the second-largest competitive nuclear fleet in the U.S. with a large natural gas dispatchable fleet and a top-tier retail electricity business. The model is designed to capture baseload scarcity value, IRA tax-credit economics, and retail margin while using an industry-leading hedging program (98% hedged for 2026) to dampen volatility. The archetype that emerges is a **“contracted-commodity cash-flow compounder”**—one that uses acquisitions and share buybacks to grow per-share metrics rather than relying solely on organic volume growth.

### Strengths (confidence weighted)
- **Hedging-driven near-term earnings visibility: HIGH confidence.** The 98% hedge coverage for 2026 and 89% for 2027 makes the 2026 guidance highly bankable by power-sector standards. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***
- **Scale and diversification across fuel types and ISOs: HIGH confidence.** ~50,000 MW pro forma for Cogentrix, spanning nuclear, gas, solar, and storage, reduces single-market concentration. [Source: Company press release / Date: 2026-01-05] ***
- **Strong FCF conversion and return-of-capital track record: HIGH confidence.** FY2025 FCFbG of $3.6B and cumulative $6.3B of buybacks since 2021 demonstrate cash generation discipline. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***; [Source: Form 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27] ***
- **Nuclear PTC and contracted PPAs: MEDIUM-HIGH confidence.** The IRA nuclear PTC and recent Meta/AWS long-term contracts provide durable, above-market revenue streams that are unusual for competitive generators. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***

### Weaknesses (confidence weighted)
- **Elevated absolute debt load and refinancing wall: HIGH confidence.** ~$19 billion of long-term debt and potential 2026–2027 maturities of $3–6 billion (depending on classification) create refinancing risk, especially if credit markets tighten. Interest expense rose to $1.2 billion in FY2025. [Source: Company FY2025 earnings / Date: 2026-02-26] ***; [Source: Fitch Ratings / Date: 2026-04-08] **
- **GAAP earnings volatility and low earnings quality: HIGH confidence.** Large unrealized derivative marks render GAAP net income an unreliable metric and can drive sharp share-price dislocations. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***
- **Retail weather sensitivity and competitive pressure: MEDIUM-HIGH confidence.** Q1 2026 demonstrated that mild weather can materially compress retail segment results in a single quarter. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings / Date: 2026-05-07] ***
- **M&A integration and execution risk at scale: MEDIUM confidence.** Energy Harbor integration appears successful, but the pending Cogentrix deal ($4B) and rapid fleet expansion test operational and systems integration capacity. [Source: Company press release / Date: 2026-01-05] ***

### Watch Items
- **Cogentrix regulatory approvals and financing:** A delay or blocked transaction would remove a 2027 earnings accretion pillar and leave capital deployment questions.
- **Moody's rating trajectory:** With S&P and Fitch at BBB-, Moody's at B2 is an outlier. A Moody's upgrade to investment grade would lower refinancing costs; a further downgrade would constrain financial flexibility.
- **Texas Supreme Court Winter Storm Uri review:** A decision allowing generator liability could establish precedent and expose Vistra to material claims.
- **2027 market curve realization:** The $7.4B–$7.8B opportunity is not guidance. If forward power and capacity prices soften, 2027 consensus will need to reset lower.
- **Nuclear PTC regulatory guidance:** Treasury regulations interpreting the IRA nuclear PTC could change the quantum or timing of recognized revenue.

## 12. Data Gaps & Limitations

The following gaps and limitations materially affected this analysis:

1. **Precise net debt figure as of March 31, 2026.** No data found for a management-reported net debt total in the Q1 2026 earnings release; search scope: Q1 2026 earnings release, 10-K FY2025, supplemental slides. The figure used (~$19.4 billion) is inferred from enterprise value and market capitalization from a financial data provider. The analytical impact is that leverage ratios are estimates rather than company-reported figures.

The analytical impact is inability to independently model the EBITDA contribution from these contracts in 2027 and beyond.

# News & Narrative Deep Research: Vistra Corp. (VST)

Research conducted: 2026-05-21

## 1. Most Significant News in the Window

The single most important event in the window is Vistra’s **first-quarter 2026 earnings release and call on May 7, 2026**, which delivered a record calendar first-quarter adjusted EBITDA of $1.494 billion (up 20% year-over-year) and GAAP net income of $1.029 billion, both comfortably ahead of consensus. [Source: Vistra Corp. press release / Title: "Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results" / Date: 2026-05-07] *** The results were driven by generation fleet performance, higher realized capacity prices in PJM, and a first full-quarter contribution from the Lotus assets acquired in late 2025. Management reaffirmed full-year 2026 guidance ranges for Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA ($6.8B–$7.6B) and Adjusted Free Cash Flow before Growth ($3.925B–$4.725B), and maintained its 2027 adjusted EBITDA midpoint opportunity of $7.4B–$7.8B. [Source: PR Newswire / Title: "Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results" / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

**Strategic contracting with Meta.** In early January 2026, Vistra signed 20-year power purchase agreements with Meta for approximately 2,600 MW of energy and capacity from its PJM nuclear fleet (Beaver Valley, Perry, and Davis-Besse). The deal includes 433 MW of uprates and extends the operating licenses of the plants by 20 years. [Source: Carbon Credits / Title: "Vistra Leverages Nuclear Partnerships with Meta and Amazon to Drive 2026 Growth" / Date: 2026-03-09] * Purchases under the agreement begin in late 2026, with full capacity ramping through 2034. [Source: Introl Blog / Title: "Meta's 6.6GW Nuclear Power Deal" / Date: 2026-02-04] 

**Cogentrix acquisition.** On January 5, 2026, Vistra announced a $4.0 billion agreement to acquire Cogentrix Energy’s 5,500-MW natural gas portfolio across PJM, ISO New England, and ERCOT. The deal is expected to close in mid-to-late 2026, subject to FERC, DOJ Hart-Scott-Rodino, and state regulatory approvals. [Source: Vistra Corp. press release / Title: "Vistra Adds to its Industry-Leading Generation Portfolio with Acquisition of Cogentrix" / Date: 2026-01-05] *** Management guided that the deal will deliver mid-single-digit accretion to Ongoing Operations Adjusted FCF per share in 2027 and high-single-digit average accretion over 2027–2029, exceeding its mid-teens levered return threshold. [Source: Power Magazine / Title: "Vistra to Bolster Gas-Fired Fleet by 5.5 GW with $4B Cogentrix Acquisition" / Date: 2026-01-14] **

**Credit upgrade to investment grade.** Fitch Ratings upgraded Vistra’s corporate issuer credit rating to investment grade during the quarter, making it the second major agency to do so following S&P’s action in 2025. The upgrade triggered fallaway provisions that released liens on senior secured debt assets. [Source: PR Newswire / Title: "Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results" / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

**Fleet stress test during Winter Storm Fern.** In late January 2026 (January 24–27), Winter Storm Fern tested grid resilience across ERCOT and PJM. Vistra reported that its natural gas fleet achieved 97% commercial availability and its nuclear fleet ran at 100% availability during the storm, allowing strong capture of tight market conditions. [Source: TipRanks / Title: "Vistra Energy Earnings Call Signals Cash-Rich Growth" / Date: 2026-05-07] * Real-time prices in ERCOT peaked around $300/MWh, while PJM averaged roughly $700/MWh system-wide. [Source: SemiAnalysis / Title: "Are AI Datacenters Increasing Electric Bills for American Households?" / Date: 2026-03-03] *

**NRC license renewal for Perry.** On May 19, 2026, the Nuclear Regulatory Commission approved the license renewal application for Vistra’s Perry nuclear plant, which had been operating under its original 40-year license set to expire in 2027. [Source: ANS Nuclear Newswire / Title: "Vistra’s Perry nuclear plant approved for license renewal" / Date: 2026-05-19] * This approval supports the Meta PPA, which assumes long-term license extensions for the PJM units.

**Market-monitor pushback on Cogentrix.** On April 8, 2026, RTO Insider reported that ISO-NE and PJM market monitors had expressed concerns that the Cogentrix acquisition would increase Vistra’s market power and could undermine competition in those regions. [Source: RTO Insider / Title: "ISO-NE, PJM Market Monitors Concerned About Vistra Acquisition of Cogentrix" / Date: 2026-04-08] *

## 2. Earnings Call Deep Dive

### Headline Takeaways
Vistra framed Q1 2026 as proof that its integrated model is operating at a "structurally higher earnings base." Generation delivered $1.426 billion of adjusted EBITDA, while Retail contributed only $68 million (down from $184 million in Q1 2025) due to exceptionally mild ERCOT weather. The company highlighted that its hedge book supports visibility: as of May 1, 2026, Vistra had hedged approximately 98% of expected 2026 generation volumes, 89% for 2027, and 65% for 2028. [Source: TipRanks / Title: "Vistra Energy Earnings Call Signals Cash-Rich Growth" / Date: 2026-05-07] *

### Management Tone & Conviction
CEO Jim Burke struck an upbeat, operationally focused tone. In prepared remarks he said: *"Vistra had an exciting start to 2026, powered by the talent of our people, the capabilities of our generation portfolio, our commitment to our customers, and our ability to grow strategically."* He added that the Fitch upgrade *"reflects the progress we've made in strengthening our balance sheet and providing visibility into the longer-term earnings power of the company."* [Source: PR Newswire / Title: "Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results" / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

On the demand outlook, Burke emphasized dispatchable generation as essential: *"Load growth remains strong across our primary markets, and we believe a large, diversified, and dispatchable generation fleet like ours is essential in meeting demand and supporting market reliability."* [Source: PR Newswire / Title: "Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results" / Date: 2026-05-07] ***

CFO Kris Moldovan characterized the retail decline as anticipated, noting that the first quarter was *"the second warmest first quarter since 1950"* in ERCOT. He stressed that the integrated model provided an offset: Generation benefited from opportunistic commercial optimization during mild periods. [Source: TIKR / Title: "Can Vistra Stock Reach $190? The Q1 Report Makes the Bull Case More Credible" / Date: 2026-05-09] 

### Q&A Pushback & Analyst Skepticism
Analysts pressed management on four main fronts:

1. **PJM policy and co-location rules.** Analysts asked whether PJM’s evolving co-location rules and interconnection reforms would slow the replication of the Meta-style nuclear contracts. Management responded that FERC has directed PJM to support co-location and that customer discussions are active, but acknowledged that *"details still need to be clarified"* and that policy uncertainty is a real execution risk. [Source: TipRanks / Title: "Vistra Energy Earnings Call Signals Cash-Rich Growth" / Date: 2026-05-07] *

2. **ERCOT forward curve weakness.** Analysts questioned why ERCOT forward power prices had softened despite robust load growth forecasts. Management argued that current forwards *"underprice the true demand outlook"* and see the disconnect as creating near-term mark-to-market pressure but longer-term upside. [Source: TipRanks / Title: "Vistra Energy Earnings Call Signals Cash-Rich Growth" / Date: 2026-05-07] *

3. **Retail segment trajectory.** With Texas retail EBITDA down sharply year-over-year, analysts asked whether full-year retail targets were at risk. Management maintained that retail remains on track to meet medium-term EBITDA goals, though performance should moderate from last year’s unusually strong levels. [Source: TipRanks / Title: "Vistra Energy Earnings Call Signals Cash-Rich Growth" / Date: 2026-05-07] *

4. **Battery/storage returns.** Management sounded notably cautious on standalone 1–2 hour battery projects without long-term offtake, stating that most such assets have generated limited value for owners so far. [Source: TipRanks / Title: "Vistra Energy Earnings Call Signals Cash-Rich Growth" / Date: 2026-05-07] *

### Forward Commentary & Guidance
Guidance was reaffirmed rather than raised, which may have disappointed some momentum-oriented investors. Importantly, both the 2026 and 2027 guidance ranges explicitly exclude any contribution from the pending Cogentrix acquisition and the Meta PPAs, leaving visible upside if those close and ramp as planned. The company also reiterated its commitment to return at least $3 billion to equity holders through 2027. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "Vistra Corp. Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary" / Date: 2026-05-07] **

## 3. Analyst Coverage & Rating Changes

### Consensus Picture
As of May 21, 2026, the stock carries a consensus "Buy" rating. Yahoo Finance data shows an average price target of $225.06, with a low of $97.00 and a high of $320.00. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: VST Stock Price, News, Quote & History / Date: 2026-05-21] ** MarketWatch data (as of the research date) shows 20 ratings with an average target of $233.06, ranging from $192.00 to $320.00, with 17 Buy, 1 Overweight, 1 Hold, and 1 Underweight rating. [Source: MarketWatch / Title: "Vistra Corp. Analyst Estimates" / Date: 2026-05-21] *

### Specific Rating Actions in the Window

[Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "JPMorgan Raises its Price Target on Vistra Corp." / Date: 2026-02-21] **

[Source: RTO Insider / Title: "ISO-NE, PJM Market Monitors Concerned About Vistra Acquisition of Cogentrix" / Date: 2026-04-08] * The full arguments are paywalled, but the headline represents the first identifiable institutional resistance to the deal and is being watched as a potential delay or conditioning risk.

[Source: ANS Nuclear Newswire / Title: "Vistra’s Perry nuclear plant approved for license renewal" / Date: 2026-05-19] * Comanche Peak Units 1 and 2 had their licenses renewed earlier, extending to 2050 and 2053 respectively. [Source: ANS Nuclear Newswire / Title: "Comanche Peak adds years to operating licenses" / Date: unknown] *

No material litigation or EPA enforcement actions were identified in the research window.

### M&A Activity
- **Cogentrix acquisition:** Announced January 5, 2026; $4.0 billion net purchase price ($2.3B cash, $0.9B stock at $185/share, assumption of ~$1.5B debt less ~$0.7B tax benefits). Expected to close in H2 2026. [Source: Vistra Corp. press release / Title: "Vistra Adds to its Industry-Leading Generation Portfolio with Acquisition of Cogentrix" / Date: 2026-01-05] ***

### Leadership Changes

### Restructuring & Workforce Actions

### Capital Return Announcements

Since November 2021, the company has executed ~$6.3 billion in buybacks, retiring ~169 million shares (~30% reduction) at an average cost of ~$37 per share. Approximately $1.5 billion of authorization remained as of May 1, 2026, expected to be completed by year-end 2027. [Source: PR Newswire / Title: "Vistra Reports First Quarter 2026 Results" / Date: 2026-05-07] ***
- **Dividend:** On April 30, 2026, Vistra declared a quarterly common dividend of $0.2290 per share (payable June 30, 2026, to holders of record June 22, 2026), implying an annualized yield of roughly 0.6% at the May 21 share price. [Source: Vistra Corp. press release / Title: "Vistra Declares Dividend on Common Stock..." / Date: 2026-04-30] ***

Burke sold 662,707 shares for an estimated $133.8 million, EVP Carrie Lee Kirby sold 58,275 shares (~$10.2M), and EVP Stephanie Zapata Moore sold 18,219 shares (~$3.0M). [Source: Quiver Quantitative / Title: "$VST Insider Trading Activity" / Date: 2026-05-21] In the immediate window, the only disclosed activity was director stock awards on May 15, 2026, with no open-market purchases or sales. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "Vistra Corp. Recent Insider Transactions" / Date: 2026-05-15] *

## 6. Sentiment & Positioning

### Institutional Sentiment
Institutional ownership stood at approximately 93.1% as of mid-May 2026, with insiders holding 0.43%. [Source: ChartMill / Title: "VISTRA CORP (VST) Stock Ownership" / Date: 2026-05-13] The top shareholders include Vanguard (~13%), BlackRock (~8.7%), and FMR (~5.8%). [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "With 86% ownership in Vistra Corp." / Date: 2025-05-06] * (note: the 86% figure is from May 2025; the more recent 93.1% reading from ChartMill is preferred). No major 13F position initiations or exits in Q1 2026 were identified beyond routine small-scale filings. Most recent as of 2026-05-13.

### Retail & Social Sentiment
Retail sentiment appears muted and not a material driver of the stock. Vistra is not among the most-discussed names on Reddit or StockTwits relative to its market cap, and no retail-driven volume spikes were identified. Marked explicitly as lower-signal than institutional positioning.

### Short Interest
Float short interest was reported at 3.35% as of May 13, 2026. [Source: ChartMill / Title: "VISTRA CORP (VST) Stock Ownership" / Date: 2026-05-13] This is a relatively low level and does not suggest significant bearish positioning. No notable short-interest expansion was identified over the window.

### Valuation Narrative
The dominant market debate centers on whether Vistra’s current earnings power is sustainable or a cyclical peak. The stock trades at a trailing P/E of ~24.9x and a forward P/E of ~15.5x (per Yahoo Finance as of May 20, 2026), a noticeable discount to its recent trading range and to sell-side price targets averaging ~$225. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: VST Stock Price, News, Quote & History / Date: 2026-05-21] ** The narrative appears split: bulls argue the market is underpricing structural load growth (5–6% annually in ERCOT, 2–3% in PJM) and long-duration contracted cash flows from Meta/Amazon; bears argue that forward power curves have softened, ERCOT interconnection queue data is inflated, and the Cogentrix deal may face regulatory friction. The stock’s 1-year return of 1.47% versus the S&P 500’s 27.39% suggests the market has moved from thematic enthusiasm to a "show me" posture. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: VST Stock Price, News, Quote & History / Date: 2026-05-21] **

## 7. Short-Seller Research & Activist Investor Involvement

No short-seller reports or activist investor activity were identified in the research window.

## 8. Narrative Shifts

A clear narrative shift has occurred over the past 12 months. **Prior narrative (2024–mid-2025):** Vistra was a high-momentum "AI power" thematic darling, with the stock surging hundreds of percent as investors priced in insatiable data-center demand for dispatchable generation and nuclear energy. **Current narrative (late 2025–present):** Despite continued operational execution—record earnings, investment-grade ratings, 20-year tech PPAs, and aggressive buybacks—the stock has flattened. The narrative has shifted from unchecked thematic enthusiasm to skepticism about the *sustainability* of power prices and the *timing* of demand realization.

The trigger appears to be a combination of **(1)** softening ERCOT forward curves and mild weather undermining the "scarcity" thesis, **(2)** policy uncertainty in PJM slowing the co-location narrative, and **(3)** the stock’s massive 2024 run leaving it vulnerable to multiple compression as rates remained higher-for-longer and growth expectations normalized. Vistra is no longer being rewarded as a pure-play AI infrastructure name; it is being scrutinized as a leveraged merchant generator with execution risk on a large M&A integration.

## 9. Narrative-vs-Reality Disconnects

**There is a material disconnect.** The market narrative appears to be pricing in significant demand disappointment and/or regulatory failure, while observable reality suggests strong execution:

- **Earnings and guidance:** The company just posted a record Q1, reaffirmed robust 2026/2027 guidance, and hedged 98% of 2026 volumes.
- **Contracted growth:** Vistra has secured 20-year PPAs with Meta (~2,600 MW) and Amazon (~1,200 MW at Comanche Peak), providing investment-grade-counterparty cash flow visibility.
- **Balance sheet/capital returns:** It achieved a second investment-grade rating, retired ~30% of shares since 2021, and is on track to return at least $3B through 2027.
- **Valuation:** Yet the stock trades at ~15.5x forward earnings with consensus EPS growth near 90% YoY for Q2 2026 and double-digit compounding through 2027, and sits ~35% below its 52-week high of $219.82. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: VST Stock Price, News, Quote & History / Date: 2026-05-21] **

The disconnect appears to reflect market skepticism that forward power prices and load growth will match management’s bullish physical outlook, particularly in ERCOT where battery additions and mild weather have dampened curves. If physical load growth materializes as projected, the current valuation embeds a significant margin of safety.

## 10. Bottom-Line Narrative Assessment

### Narrative Characterization
Vistra is currently viewed as a cash-rich, execution-dependent power generator sitting at the intersection of AI-driven load growth and merchant power volatility. The market acknowledges its operational strength and contracted nuclear wins but is no longer willing to pay a thematic premium until it sees sustained upward revisions to guidance and successful closure of the Cogentrix deal.

### Key Narrative Drivers (confidence weighted)
1. **Data-center load growth and nuclear PPA monetization** — High confidence. The Meta and Amazon deals are signed, and management has guided to 5–6% annual ERCOT load growth through 2030. These are widely held expectations, though the *timing* of revenue recognition is debated.
2. **Cogentrix acquisition closure and integration** — Medium-High confidence. The deal is expected to close H2 2026, but the April market-monitor objections introduce execution risk that is not fully priced.
3. **ERCOT forward curve and power price realization** — Medium confidence. Management insists forwards underprice physical reality; the market disagrees. This is the central point of contention.
4. **Capital return velocity (buybacks + dividend)** — High confidence. The $1.5B remaining authorization and commitment to $3B+ through 2027 are explicit and trackable.
5. **Regulatory/policy headwinds in PJM** — Medium confidence. FERC support for co-location is a tailwind, but PJM rulemaking uncertainty is a real friction point for new contracts.

### Narrative Risks (confidence weighted)
1. **FERC/DOJ conditions or delay of Cogentrix** — Medium-High risk. Market-monitor opposition could lead to asset divestitures or timeline slippage, undermining 2027 accretion math.
2. **ERCOT demand growth fails to materialize** — Medium risk. If data-center projects stall or interconnection queue data proves inflated, forward earnings power contracts.
3. **Retail segment deterioration beyond weather** — Low-Medium risk. Q1’s $68M retail EBITDA (down from $184M YoY) was blamed on mild weather, but sustained margin compression would damage the integrated model thesis.
4. **Nuclear operational outage or license setback** — Low risk. The fleet ran at 100% availability in Q1 and Perry’s renewal was approved, but Davis-Besse has a history of regulatory scrutiny.

### Catalysts to Watch
- **Q2 2026 earnings:** Expected August 6, 2026. Will show summer peak performance and updated retail trends.
- **Cogentrix regulatory milestones:** FERC Section 203 approval and HSR clearance; any additional market-monitor filings.
- **Summer 2026 ERCOT peak demand/weather:** Real-time scarcity pricing and reserve margins will test the demand-growth thesis.
- **Nuclear uprate progress:** Meta-funded 433 MW uprate timeline updates.
- **Ex-dividend date:** June 22, 2026.
- **PJM stakeholder process on co-location rules:** Expected rulemaking developments in Q3 2026.

## 11. Data Gaps & Limitations

- **Full earnings call transcript:** Access to the complete Q&A transcript was limited to paywalled sources (Seeking Alpha, GuruFocus). Summary coverage from TipRanks, TIKR, and statementdog was used, but verbatim analyst questions and management responses beyond the quoted passages could not be independently verified.
- **Market-monitor objections to Cogentrix:** The RTO Insider article (April 8, 2026) is paywalled; only the headline and lede were accessible. The specific legal or economic arguments raised by ISO-NE and PJM monitors could not be fully summarized.
- **Current options positioning:** Options data (put/call ratios) is stale or conflicting across sources (AlphaQuery dated March 20, 2026; Fintel shows 1.52 with no explicit date). No reliable May 2026 options snapshot was found.
- **13F institutional flows:** Most 13F filings identified in searches referenced Q4 2025 or earlier. Specific Q1 2026 institutional accumulation/distribution data was not available as of the research date.
- **Retail sentiment quantification:** No reliable sentiment index or social volume data for VST was found; the assessment that retail sentiment is muted is qualitative.
- **Dividend history precision:** The Q1 2026 dividend of $0.2290 per share appears to be flat or marginally below the prior $0.23 rate (rounding differences), but primary sources did not provide a clear prior-quarter comparison figure.
